steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Goldschmied et al. reanalyzed Attrill et al.'s data and found "no evidence that red-uniformed teams are more likely than other teams to win." This directly undermines Attrill et al.'s explanation that red caused Manchester United's success. The blank must complete the sentence in a way that follows logically from this finding: if red had no effect, then the uniform color was not responsible for their record.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — would probably have remained roughly the same if its players had not been wearing red uniforms.: if red gives no advantage, removing it would not have changed the outcome — directly follows from Goldschmied et al.'s finding. ✓
  • Choice B — was likely influenced by its opponents' associations with the color red.: this still attributes Manchester United's success to the color red affecting opponents, which Goldschmied et al.'s finding contradicts. ✗
  • Choice C — was directly tied to its players' general satisfaction with their uniform color.: introduces player satisfaction as a factor — nothing in the passage supports this. ✗
  • Choice D — may have influenced other teams to change their uniform colors.: off-topic; the passage is about whether red caused Manchester United's success, not about other teams' decisions. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
